title: CXXRTL edge detector not working for non-input clocks
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.22 (git sha1 f109fa3d4c5, gcc 11.3.0 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

```verilog
module top (input logic clk);
    logic[7:0] counter;
    logic clkdiv;
    always_ff @(posedge clk) clkdiv <= ~clkdiv;
    always_ff @(posedge clkdiv) counter <= counter + 1;
endmodule
```
export to c++ with `write_cxxrtl`, simulate with trivial testbench toggleing `clk` every cycle.


### Expected Behavior

`clkdiv` toggles at half the rate of `clk`.
`counter` increases at every `posedge` of `clkdiv`

### Actual Behavior

`counter` does not increase at all.
It seems this is related to the clock detection `posedge_p_clkdiv`.
It is called in `p_top::eval` at the very beginning, before a new value for `p_clkdiv.next` has been calculated.
By the next time `p_top::eval` is called `p_clkdiv` is already `commit`ed, thus missing the posedge.
